newsSince: Modestly positive: On June 15, 2026 Itron announced a deal with Watercare Services (NZ's largest water utility) to deploy 100,000 Intelis digital water meters within a broader plan to connect nearly half a million smart meters across Auckland — one of NZ's largest smart-water upgrades. Reinforces the grid/water-analytics attach thesis; consistent with FY26 revenue guide of $2.35-2.45B. No negative developments.

sources.label: Itron global smart-water push (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-power-bottleneck lens: Itron's grid-edge telemetry/demand-response thesis is core to this page. The Auckland 100k-meter water win is a real but incremental design proof point (+1), supporting the high-conviction rank-4 standing without warranting a bigger move.
verdicts.bull: AMI 2.0 replacement cycle, grid-modernization spend, water/energy analytics attach; Auckland 500k-meter program adds proof.
verdicts.neutral: Single regional water deal is incremental, not transformational, to a $3.6B-cap, $2.4B-revenue base.
verdicts.bear: -13.7% YTD, utility capex timing risk; AI-power linkage is a derived rather than direct beneficiary.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AMI 2.0 cycle and grid-edge software attach driving margins.
